title: hieracium caesium subsp. caesiomurorum
intro: Hieracium caesium subsp. caesiomurorum, also known as caesium hawkweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion family that is native to the Iberian Peninsula. This plant has been used for its medicinal properties, primarily as a diuretic, and has been known to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Hieracium caesium subsp. caesiomurorum include its potential to treat urinary tract issues, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of various gastrointestinal disorders. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to address a range of health concerns.
